title: Indulgent Ikasumi Okinawan Tofu Sushi-Style Chirashi
description: A savory, silky Okinawan-inspired dish featuring tofu, ikasumi seaweed, and pickled red onions, all served in a delicate cedar plank vessel.

ingredients.name: Cedars planks (4)
ingredients.amount: 1/4 inch thick, 6-8 inches long, 2-3 inches wide

instructions: Soak the cedar planks in water for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and cook the tofu until golden brown on all sides.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, grated ginger, and sesame seeds.
Add the pickled red onions, sliced red onions, and chopped scallions to the bowl and stir to combine.
Place a cedar plank on a flat surface and spoon a layer of the tofu mixture onto the plank.
Arrange a few pieces of ikasumi seaweed on top of the tofu and drizzle with the soy sauce mixture.
Garnish with chopped scallions and a sprinkle of sesame seeds.
Repeat the layers two more times, finishing with a layer of tofu on top.
Note: The chirashi can be assembled and refrigerated for up to 24 hours before serving.
When ready to serve, let the chirashi come to room temperature and garnish with additional scallions and sesame seeds, if desired.
